title: Lahmacun-Style Keema Flatbread

subs.name: whole wheat flour (atta) — dough will be less extensible but still works
finely crumbled soya granules (soaked) for a vegetarian version
subs.notes: add 1 tsp extra oil to the topping

steps.text: Mix yeast with warm water and a pinch of sugar. Leave 5 minutes until foamy. Combine with flour and salt in a bowl, knead for 5 minutes into a smooth dough. Rest covered for 15 minutes.

While the dough rests, make the topping: combine the keema, minced onion, diced tomato, capsicum, garlic, chilli powder, cumin, coriander and salt in a bowl. Squeeze the mixture firmly together with your hands until it becomes a uniform paste — this is critical for it to stick to the dough.
Divide the dough into 4 balls. On a floured surface, roll each ball as thin as possible — about 2 mm — into a roughly oval or round shape. The thinner the dough, the crispier the result.
Spread a thin, even layer of the raw meat topping over each flatbread, pressing it gently into the dough. Use about 4 tablespoons of topping per round — too thick and it won't cook through.

Heat a dry tawa on medium-high flame. Place the lahmacun topping-side up. Cover with a lid and cook for 4–5 minutes until the base is charred in spots and the meat topping has changed colour and cooked through.
Transfer to a board. Scatter parsley over the top, squeeze lemon over it, roll into a cylinder and eat immediately. Repeat for remaining flatbreads.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
Fermentation conditions affect safety. Discard batches with unexpected mould, putrid odour or other spoilage signs.
critical_checks: Use an appropriate food thermometer and safe doneness guidance for the specific cut of meat.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: keema → finely crumbled soya granules (soaked) for a vegetarian version (add 1 tsp extra oil to the topping); maida → whole wheat flour (atta) — dough will be less extensible but still works (roll slightly thicker); parsley → fresh coriander leaves (a common home adaptation)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Keema (minced lamb or beef) is available at butchers across India. Cook on a hot cast-iron tawa with the lid on — the covered steam helps cook the meat topping through. Serve with sliced onion soaked in lemon juice, mimicking the Turkish accompaniment.
